sanskrit: आयुधानामहं वज्रं धेनूनामस्मि कामधुक्। प्रजनश्चास्मि कन्दर्पः सर्पाणामस्मि वासुकिः॥
transliteration: āyudhānām ahaṃ vajraṃ dhenūnāmasmi kāmadhuk | prajanaścāsmi kandarpaḥ sarpāṇāmasmi vāsukiḥ ||
literal_meaning: Among weapons I am the thunderbolt; among cows I am the wish-fulfilling cow; among progenitors I am Kandarpa (Cupid); among serpents I am Vāsuki.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names four potent symbols—irresistible force, boundless nourishment, creative desire, and transformative energy—to show that every domain of life contains a supreme, conscious potential that can be aligned with the Self rather than feared or repressed.
psychological_state: A moment of cognitive re-framing: the client realizes that impulses he judges as dangerous (sex, anger, instinctive drives) are not to be eliminated but recognized as carriers of divine potency when consciously harnessed.
trigger_scenario: A high-achieving professional who fears his own ambition and libido enters therapy after an affair and burnout; he must learn to own his ‘thunderbolt’ energy and erotic life without letting them control him.
anchor_text: See divine power in force, nurture, desire, and transformation.
